assessment.summary: A present, quantified toll of hundreds of thousands of conflict-related deaths a year across dozens of countries, with fatalities and the count of major wars both trending upward — carrying a real risk of wider regional escalation.
assessment.timeframe: Chronic and ongoing, with conflict-related fatalities at their highest annual level in years and the number of major conflicts rising.

claims.text: The number of locations of armed conflict fell slightly, from 51 states in 2023 to 49 in 2024, while the estimated overall number of fatalities rose from 188,000 in 2023 to 239,000 in 2024 — the highest annual total in the period 2018-24 (SIPRI Yearbook 2025).

There were five major armed conflicts involving over 10,000 estimated conflict-related fatalities in 2024, one more than in 2023: the Israel-Hamas and Russia-Ukraine wars, the civil wars in Myanmar and Sudan, and the subnational conflict in Ethiopia (SIPRI Yearbook 2025).
The number of high-intensity conflicts, with 1,000-9,999 estimated conflict-related fatalities, fell from 20 in 2023 to 19 in 2024 (SIPRI Yearbook 2025).

description: Ongoing state-based and civil wars that already kill hundreds of thousands annually and displace tens of millions more, with conflict-related fatalities and the number of major wars trending upward in recent years. Escalation, spillover between neighboring conflicts, and breakdown of humanitarian norms are the primary pathways to wider regional or global instability.
name: Global Armed Conflict
